habitat: Hill and lowland dipterocarp forests; dry ridges; hilltops; limestone areas; and secondary forest
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Bornean Banded Pitta (Hydrornis schwaneri). Classified under the Pittidae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Hill and lowland dipterocarp forests; dry ridges; hilltops; limestone areas; and secondary forest. The current IUCN classification of Least Concern (LC) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Bornean Banded Pitta is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Bornean Banded Pitta, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
